title: Obfuscation/backdoor detection config
description: Per-language config consumed by generic Layer-B detectors. Adding a language means adding one of these files; no detector code changes are required.

aliases.description: Alternate names users might map to (e.g. 'sh' for bash).

filenames.description: Exact filenames that imply this language (e.g. 'Dockerfile').

dynamic_exec_sinks.description: Fully qualified callee names that execute their first non-literal argument. Matched as dotted paths after import/alias resolution.

decoders.description: Functions that turn opaque bytes/strings back into executable text or bytecode (base64, hex, gunzip, decrypt, unmarshal).

deserializers.description: Functions that materialize attacker-controlled objects/code (pickle.loads, marshal.loads, Marshal.load, ObjectInputStream.readObject).

network_io.description: Outbound network primitives (HTTP fetch, sockets) — sources for network-then-execute flows.

secrets_io.description: Reads of credentials, environment, keychains, browser stores.

shell_exec.description: OS command execution primitives. Distinct from in-process eval sinks.

library_load.description: In-memory or dynamic library/assembly loaders (Reflection.Assembly.Load, ctypes.CDLL, plugin.Open, dlopen).

obfuscator_id_patterns.description: Regexes that match identifier names produced by common obfuscators in this language.

min_mean_identifier_length.description: Files whose mean identifier length falls below this threshold are flagged (after excluding minified paths).

obfuscator_id_min_ratio.description: Ratio of obfuscator-pattern identifiers to total identifiers required to flag.

string_concat_operators.description: Operator tokens used to concatenate strings; helps detect split sink names like 'ev'+'al'.

language_specific_detectors.description: IDs of language-specific detectors to enable for this language (see detectors/lang-specific/).

manifests.description: Manifest detector IDs that apply to this ecosystem (e.g. 'package-json', 'setup-py').
